mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during recovery periods, with a hit rate below 45 percent over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly increases corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of quarters following positive GDP growth revisions across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60 percent of months where inflation exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold during high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports higher consumer income and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts signal renewed confidence in the residential construction sector and broader economic stabilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during post-crisis recovery environments.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases during identified recovery periods over a 10-year historical backtest.
